name: PowerShell Abuse
description: An encoded PowerShell command downloads and runs a payload in memory, avoiding files on disk.

stages.name: Encoded launch
stages.detail: Hidden window, no profile, base64 command to evade casual inspection.

stages.name: Download cradle
stages.detail: Fetches a script from C2 and runs it in memory (fileless).

stages.name: In-memory execution
stages.detail: Runs the payload without ever writing to disk.

events.message: Process Create: powershell.exe encoded command, parent WINWORD.EXE

name: Process Hollowing
description: A benign process is started suspended, its image is replaced with malicious code, then resumed.

stages.name: Create suspended
stages.detail: A legitimate process is created but not yet running.

stages.name: Unmap & write
stages.detail: The original image is removed and a malicious PE is written in.

stages.name: Hijack thread
stages.detail: The entry point is redirected to malicious code; the process resumes as malware.

events.message: Process Create: notepad.exe (no window, unusual parent powershell.exe)
ProcessAccess: powershell.exe opened notepad.exe with PROCESS_VM_WRITE

name: Credential Dumping (LSASS)
description: LSASS memory is dumped to extract NTLM hashes and Kerberos tickets.

stages.name: Open LSASS
stages.detail: Requests read access to LSASS memory.

stages.name: Dump memory
stages.detail: Writes LSASS memory to a file on disk.

stages.name: Extract secrets
stages.detail: Parses the dump for hashes and tickets.

name: Kerberoasting
description: An authenticated user requests service tickets for SPNs and cracks them offline for service-account passwords.

stages.name: Enumerate SPNs
stages.detail: Finds accounts that have Service Principal Names.

stages.name: Request tickets
stages.action: Request RC4 TGS for target SPNs
stages.detail: The KDC returns service tickets encrypted with the account's hash.

stages.name: Crack offline
stages.detail: Recovers the service-account password offline.

events.message: Kerberos service ticket requested (RC4 0x17) for many SPNs by one user

name: Registry Persistence
description: Malware adds a Run key so it launches at every logon.

stages.name: Write Run value
stages.detail: Adds an autostart entry pointing at the payload.

stages.name: Persist
stages.detail: The payload runs on every subsequent logon.

events.message: Process Create: powershell.exe modifying a Run key

name: Reverse Shell
description: A payload connects back to attacker infrastructure for interactive command execution.

stages.name: Establish socket
stages.detail: Outbound connection to the attacker.

stages.name: Bind shell
stages.detail: The attacker receives an interactive shell.

stages.name: Command exec
stages.detail: Full interactive control of the host.

name: DNS Tunneling
description: Data is exfiltrated and C2 is carried inside DNS queries.

stages.name: Encode data
stages.detail: Splits data across many DNS labels.

stages.name: Query loop
stages.detail: High-volume TXT/A queries.

stages.name: Reassemble
stages.action: Attacker DNS server rebuilds the data
stages.detail: Exfiltration over an allowed protocol.

events.message: DNS Query: hundreds of TXT queries to *.exfil.attacker.com by svch0st.exe

name: Ransomware
description: Backups are destroyed and files are encrypted for impact.

stages.name: Inhibit recovery
stages.detail: Removes Volume Shadow Copies to block recovery.

stages.name: Kill services
stages.detail: Stops processes that lock target files.

stages.name: Encrypt
stages.action: Enumerate and encrypt files; drop ransom note
stages.detail: Data is encrypted and a ransom note is written.

events.message: Process Create: Invoice_2026.pdf.exe (double extension) from Downloads
